Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: spawn node <real-script> instead of claude symlink to avoid posix_spawnp failure

node-pty's posix_spawnp cannot execute symlinks or shebang scripts directly.
The claude binary is /opt/homebrew/bin/claude → cli.js (a Node.js script).

Fix:
- Resolve NODE_BIN and CLAUDE_SCRIPT to their real paths at startup (fs.realpathSync)
- Spawn node directly: pty.spawn(NODE_BIN, [CLAUDE_SCRIPT, '-p', prompt, ...])
- This bypasses the symlink/shebang issue entirely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace max-height animation with transform in sk-collapse

sk-collapse was animating max-height, which triggers reflow on every
frame. Now uses transform: scaleY(0) — compositor-only, no layout
recalculation. Removes useLayoutEffect height measurement from
FadeTransition.

Also adds layout-property animation ban to Stylelint factory
(transition on width, height, margin, padding, top/right/bottom/left).

commit.message:
Add modern, developer-friendly protocol specifications

Created two comprehensive specification documents:

1. NEC-7142-SPEC.md (1200+ lines)
   - Complete TCP/7142 protocol reference
   - Packet framing, BCC checksums, ASCII-hex encoding
   - VCP commands (get/set/persist) with ranges
   - CTL commands (power, settings, scheduling, identity)
   - Session lifecycle, error handling, cooldowns
   - Quirks: broadcast limits, input bounce, firmware behavior
   - Quick-start examples and full API reference

2. MEDIA-PLAYER-CGI-SPEC.md (1000+ lines)
   - HTTP CGI API for media player folder/file management
   - Critical firmware quirks clearly documented:
     * insecureHTTPParser: true requirement
     * FM= prerequisite before filelist.json
     * Trailing comma JSON bug workaround
     * Path encoding asymmetry (V=S,2B critical notes)
   - Navigation (FL), file manager (FM), folder ops (FC/FD)
   - File upload (Fu), finalization (RSG), restart (RSB)
   - Settings (V=S for autoplay, interval) with encoding guides
   - Complete playback workflow with code examples
   - Quirks: FM state resets, index-only navigation, timing

Both specs replace scattered CLAUDE.md documentation with
clean, modern formats. Include:
- Quick-start code examples
- Request/response formats
- Parameter encoding guides
- JavaScript integration patterns
- Firmware quirks and workarounds
- Testing notes for simulator

These serve as permanent reference for any developer
integrating with NEC large-format displays and their
bundled media players.

commit.message:
docs(agent): comprehensive design elaboration — intents, routing, fault tolerance, CLI

Fixes and clarifications:
- Escalation table: replace `document` with `requirements` + `plan` (both are always Claude)
- LLMProvider interface: add ToolDefinition/ToolCall/ToolResult/LLMResponse types and tools param
- Implement outcome handling: explicit fix-direction step from Claude; plan_step_update on APPROVED
- Test fix loop: add claudeRounds cap and surface-to-user terminal condition
- Model config: split document.escalation → document.review + document.cross_cutting roles
- Web search table: clarify Claude Code WebSearch vs raw web_search_20260209 type string
- Cross-session seeding: replace invalid search('') with embedded vector query
- Ollama context window: attributed to model (qwen3-coder), not Ollama itself
- Map.groupBy: note Node.js ≥ 21 requirement; fix search('') → query() in maintenance job

New sections:
- Intent classification: keyword map, graph signal tie-breaking, @claude/@opus/@local/@local prefixes
- @opus escalation: model tier table, cost announcement callout before Opus calls
- plan_step_update in debug: plan step lifecycle when debugging under an active plan
- Fault tolerance: Ollama unavailability (4 scenarios), daemon crash/stale graph (4 scenarios)
- Plan pre-flight: local condensed sketch before Claude enhancement; fallback paths
- Token budget overflow priority: ordered drop table (L4→L3b→L3a→L2, never L1)
- CLI/REPL interface: REPL commands, prefix table, one-shot CLI mode, /cost /status /forget
- Re-indexing handoff: file-scoped background re-index after implement/refactor writes
- Multi-step plan execution UX: turn header, step continuation, success/failure transitions

commit.message:
Fix byte immediate reduction to mask value before sign conversion

Previously, the byte immediate fixup only subtracted 256 from values
>= 128, without first masking to the low 8 bits. This caused incorrect
results for values larger than 255 (e.g., 5369233654 was not properly
truncated to its low byte before sign extension).

commit.message:
Self-heal leaderboard for users missing due to prior rules rejection

When a user opens the mitzvah section and has a stored completion but is
not present in the loaded leaderboard (e.g. their prior write was rejected
by Firestore rules that blocked firstCompletedAt/lastCompletedAt), silently
recalculate their total from mitzvahProgress and refresh the display.

This fixes Bar and any other user whose leaderboard entry was lost due to
the hasOnly rules mismatch, without requiring them to resubmit a reflection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: gravity well health score includes all metrics

Score now combines 7 factors with progressive threshold curves:
- Latency vs threshold (20%): stepped curve, accelerates at 80%+
- Uptime deficit (15%): sqrt curve, small loss = visible
- Packet loss (10%): from sparkline data
- CPU vs threshold (15%): matched via Proxmox guest → PingHost
- RAM vs threshold (15%): same matching
- Disk vs threshold (10%): same matching
- Syslog errors (10%): error/critical/alert count per host (24h)
- Integration health (5%): flat penalty if integration is down

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: simplify HA setup + remove wake reconnect race condition

Root cause: coordinator async_setup blocked 25s+ (sleep + wake + commands),
causing HA setup timeout → all entities unavailable. wake() reconnect
logic while listener active caused race conditions (dead connection).

Coordinator: match pre-map pattern (446be16) — commands before listener,
try/except around each, no blocking wake. Fast poll backfills if asleep.

Client wake(): remove WS reconnect (keepalive loop handles escalation).
Just send bursts and wait — no more listener race conditions.

Vacuum _ensure_awake(): simplified — no broadcast staleness check,
no exception on wake failure, command attempted regardless.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix desktop chart lag: crosshair tooltip via direct DOM instead of state

The crosshair move handler was calling setCrosshairData/setCrosshairPoint
on every mouse movement (60+ fps), triggering full React re-renders of
the 3400-line component including all detail sections. Replaced with
useRef + direct DOM manipulation so tooltip updates bypass React entirely.

commit.message:
replace network-exporter pull scraping with OTel push via overlay

Add an OTel Collector sidecar on cherryblossom (host networking) that
scrapes network-exporter locally and pushes metrics to VictoriaMetrics
via OTLP through the Tailscale overlay. This eliminates the need for
VictoriaMetrics to scrape the cherryblossom host Tailscale IP directly.

- Add OTel Collector to cherryblossom monitoring stack
- Add :8428 VictoriaMetrics forward to juul's TS_SERVE_CONFIG
- Remove network scrape job from prometheus.yml
- Remove cherryblossom-host extra_hosts from VictoriaMetrics
- Remove CHERRYBLOSSOM_HOST_IP env var

commit.message:
feat(api,ui): applications multi-env display + FAPI key management (CAB-1748) (#1566)

## Summary
- **Phase 1**: Applications page loads ALL apps globally (no env
dependency), groups by environment with tabs
(All/Production/Staging/Development) — same UX pattern as Gateways page
- **Phase 2**: FAPI profiles now support two key modes: Upload PEM/JWK
(inline conversion to JWKS) or JWKS URL. Security profile radio selector
added to create form
- Alembic migration 061: `jwks_data` JSONB column on
`portal_applications`
- 10 unit tests for `jwks_utils.py` (PEM→JWK conversion, input parsing)

## Changes
### Backend (`control-plane-api`)
- `services/jwks_utils.py` — PEM→JWK conversion (RSA + EC P-256),
`parse_jwks_input()` for PEM/JWK/JWKS
- `routers/portal_applications.py` — Accept `jwks` field, validate FAPI
requires `jwks_uri` OR `jwks`, store inline JWKS
- `services/keycloak_service.py` — Inject `jwt.credential.public.key`
attribute for FAPI clients
- `models/portal_application.py` — Add `jwks_data` JSONB column
- `routers/applications.py` — Expose `environment` and
`security_profile` in tenant apps response

### Frontend (`control-plane-ui`)
- `pages/Applications.tsx` — Multi-env tabs, security profile selector,
FAPI key toggle (Upload PEM / JWKS URL)
- `types/index.ts` — `SecurityProfile` type, `jwks`/`jwks_uri` fields on
`ApplicationCreate`
- `services/api.ts` — Remove environment param from `getApplications()`

## Test plan
- [x] 10 unit tests pass (`test_jwks_utils.py`)
- [x] ESLint 103/105 warnings
- [x] Prettier clean
- [x] TypeScript clean
- [x] Ruff clean
- [ ] CI green

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Batch gap checker S3 reads by hour to avoid connection limits

hour-level batches, each processing its CSVs and parquet files in a

fast single-query path.

commit.message:
Add AI-powered weekly planner for proactive staffing and ordering

- New weeklyPlanner Lambda analyzes historical day-of-week patterns,
  trend multipliers, and inventory levels to generate 7-day plans
- Smart staffing: rounds to whole people with 2-person minimum crew,
  uses revenue-per-labor-hour ratios from actual timeclock data
- Order recommendations with urgency levels based on projected usage
- Waste pattern alerts by day-of-week and top waste ingredients
- Configurable safety buffer (5-25%) applies differently to staffing
  (whole-person rounding) vs ordering (percentage-based)
- Data quality assessment with confidence messaging for thin data
- Optional AI insights via Bedrock for actionable manager advice
- WeeklyPlanScreen with expandable day cards, buffer selector,
  order recommendations, waste alerts, and data quality banner
- Added to Management hub, accessible to manager/owner roles only
- Expanded assistant data window to 180 days for trend questions

commit.message:
Formalize arena boundary protocol with __promote_*_inline__ callbacks

Replace hand-rolled field traversal at every boundary site with generated
per-struct __promote_*_inline__ functions — the single definition of how
to promote a struct's handle fields at boundary exit. This deletes ~420
lines of duplicated promotion logic (code_gen_stmt_func_promote.c,
code_gen_util_promote.c) and replaces it with the callback system.

Key changes:
- Add code_gen_boundary.c/h: formalized boundary enter/exit/self-promote
- Generate __promote_*_inline__ with source-guarded field promotion
- Generate __promote_array_*__ for array element field promotion
- Generate __cleanup_array_*__ for ownership-guarded element cleanup
- Add arena != dest guard to skip redundant array element iteration
  when the copy callback already handled elements during promote
- Add rt_array_promote_handle_elements() for simple handle arrays
- Delete code_gen_stmt_func_promote.c/h and code_gen_util_promote.c

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix infinite loop caused by worker's claude -p retriggering Stop hook

The flashcard worker spawns `claude -p` to generate cards. When that
session ends, it fires the Stop hook again, creating an endless chain
of worker processes (each delayed by the debounce timer). Fix by setting
AMBER_FLASHCARD_WORKER=1 before the claude -p call and checking for it
in the hook entry point.

Bumps version to 0.9.1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
